discussion_comments.comment_body:
I don't think this can be a ref unfortunately, it needs to be a state. It's being read in renders further down in context consumers, and it's not safe to read refs in render.

The reason is that with concurrent features, we want this to reflect the correct queries being hydrated _in this fork of the world_, we don't want to have the same state for the different concurrent realities so to speak.
